senses.definition: a respectful title meaning 'sir' or 'master' used in parts of South Asia, especially in Pakistan and northern India, often referring to a person in authority or a male servant in a household
examples: He greeted the saheb with a bow.
The samosas were served by the saheb and his staff.

senses.definition: plural of saheb; multiple people addressed as saheb
examples: The sahebs were seated in the waiting room.
Several sahebs discussed the arrangements.

senses.definition: an exclamation or variant transliteration often used in maritime or regional contexts; may function as a sigh or interjection in certain transcriptions
examples: He let out a sahh of relief after the test.
The old manuscript uses sahh as a breathy exclamation.

senses.definition: a respectful term for a man in South Asia, often meaning 'sir' or 'master'; historically used for European or high-status men in colonial India
examples: The sahib walked through the bazaar.
The term sahib is still used in some languages to address men not of the local caste.

senses.definition: a respectful term for a woman in South Asia, equivalent to 'lady' or 'mistress'; used in some languages to address a woman with status
examples: The sahib and sahiba hosted the guests.
The word sahiba appears in traditional stories.

senses.definition: variant spelling of sahiba; a respectful female title in some South Asian languages
examples: She was addressed as sahibah in the household records.

senses.definition: plural of sahibah; multiple female holders of the title
examples: The sahibahs gathered for a festival.

senses.definition: plural of sahiba; female equivalents of the sahib title
examples: The sahibas shared stories of their travels.

senses.definition: plural of sahib; several men addressed as sahib; a respectful title for men in South Asia
examples: The sahibs relaxed after the long journey.
Different sahibs shared their opinions at the meeting.

senses.definition: relating to the Sahidic dialect of the Coptic language
examples: A text in Sahidic was studied by linguists.
Sahidic manuscript details were translated for analysis.

senses.definition: a breed of cattle or a city/region name in Pakistan; also a town in Punjab
examples: Sahiwal cattle are known for good milk production.
We visited Sahiwal during our trip.

senses.definition: plural of Sahiwal
examples: The herds moved past the Sahiwals.
Sahiwals is used to refer to people from that region as well.

senses.definition: a mineral or rock type name; used in geological contexts
examples: The sample showed sahlite mineral grains.
Geologists identified sahlite in the core sample.

senses.definition: a transliteration variant; could be a surname or place name in certain languages
examples: Sahme is listed as a surname in some records.

senses.definition: a dust or seasonal term in some languages; may also refer to a place name or people group in Africa or the Middle East depending on transcription
examples: In some contexts, saho refers to a language cluster.

senses.definition: a surname or place name variant in some regions
examples: The surname sahoukar appears in records from the region.

senses.definition: plural form of sahra, which can mean 'desert' in some languages or a personal name; in English usage it may appear in transliterations
examples: The sahras stretched beyond the horizon.

senses.definition: a large cactus species native to the Sonoran Desert, also spelled saguaro
examples: The sahuaro stretches high into the desert sky.
Tourists photograph the tall sahuaro at sunset.

senses.definition: plural of sahuaro
examples: Lit by the dawn, the sahuaros stood like silent sentinels.

senses.definition: a surname or occupational title used in South Asian contexts; can refer to a moneylender or trader historically
examples: The sahukar mediated credit for the village.

senses.definition: a common given name in South Asia; also used as a affectionate title for spiritual figures (e.g., Sai Baba)
examples: Many followers call him Sai.
Sai is a popular name in the region.

senses.definition: a young rainbow trout; a culinary term used in some cuisines
examples: We grilled a saibling for dinner.
Saibling is a delicacy in Nordic countries.

senses.definition: plural of saibling
examples: The menu offered several saiblings from local waters.

senses.definition: an abbreviation for the Society for the Advancement of Information Conflict; could refer to a fictional or niche organization in some texts
examples: The report cited SAIC as a collaborator.

senses.definition: household or dialectal term; may appear as a surname or transliteration in some languages
examples: A rare usage of saice appeared in the register.
